cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the given statements is NOT an advantage of the algorithm mentioned in the passage. I need to look at each option and compare it with the text to see which one is not an advantage. First, let me recap the passage. The MIT algorithm is designed to monitor deceleration and distance from a light to predict which cars might break the Highway Code. It can give a two-second warning if a car is about to cross a crossroads, allowing other drivers to brake. The algorithm was tested in Virginia and accurately identified violators a couple of seconds before the red light. It has fewer false alarms compared to other models, which is a plus for driver guidance systems. The paper is going to be published in IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems. Now, looking at the options: A) It will raise the alarm when the driver breaks the traffic rules. The passage says the monitor "guesses" which cars are likely to break the Highway Code. It also mentions providing a warning if someone is about to shoot across a crossroads. So the algorithm does raise an alarm when a driver is likely to break the rules. So A seems to be an advantage. But wait, is it when the driver actually breaks the rules, or when the algorithm predicts they will?
